title: Expense approval
description: Research example for deciding whether an employee expense may be automatically approved.

decision.intent: Determine whether an employee expense may be automatically approved, rejected, or sent to a human reviewer.
decision.question: What approval path should this expense follow?

evidenceRequirements.description: An itemized receipt for the expense.

sources.title: Example expense policy

citation.excerpt: Expenses above 5000 require finance review. Prohibited categories must be rejected.

sources.rights: Example content; not an operational policy.

outcomes.label: Approve
outcomes.description: The evidence and declared rules support automatic approval.

outcomes.label: Manual review
outcomes.description: An accountable finance reviewer must decide.

outcomes.label: Reject
outcomes.description: The expense falls in a prohibited category.

rules.description: Reject expenses in a prohibited category.
rules.rationale: The example policy prohibits these categories.

rules.description: Send expenses above the declared threshold to finance review.
rules.rationale: The example policy requires finance review above 5000.

rules.description: Approve a supported ordinary expense at or below the threshold.
rules.rationale: Ordinary supported expenses may follow the automatic path.

exceptions.description: An expense connected to an active investigation requires human review.

escalation.message: Review the expense, cited policy, missing evidence, and conflicting rules.
